client’s guardianship. 
 • Though the client was comfortable staying in 
India, he wishes to return to the U.S. and start 
working there. Both parents want the client to 
live freely and as per his wish, regardless of 
where he wishes to stay. 
Recommendations 
 • Parents are recommended to attend parenting 
skills training by a qualified family therapist at least 
twice a month, in-person or online. 
 • If parental conflicts continue, the client should 
be offered supervised care by a recognised 
institution in the U.S. to focus on building 
independent living skills and autonomy of the 
client as he wishes to live in the U.S. in the future. 
[2025] 3 S.C.R.  
401
Sharmila Velamur v. V. Sanjay and Ors.
 • The focus of the care has to be on nurturing the 
skills and autonomy of the client in an environment 
where he feels comfortable and familiar.”
3.24 Finally, after completing all the assessments, on 06.02.2025, 
NIMHANS, Bengaluru prepared the Comprehensive Assessment
